methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Hypersonix Launch Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $46M AUD to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public defense technology firms, with primary peer reference to three same-stage companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy for current valuation, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $92M. The peer-median method references the three identified same-sector, same-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $300M (range: $150M–$500M). The disparity between methods reflects uncertainty in whether Hypersonix commands peer-level momentum or reflects the earlier-stage capital intensity of its business model.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $78M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $196M. Key uncertainties include Hypersonix's technology differentiation relative to Stark Defence and Vatn Systems, execution risk on development timelines, and the degree to which early-stage capital deployment translates to market positioning in a capital-intensive sector. The wide spread between methods (78M–345M) signals material sensitivity to peer selection and growth trajectory assumptions.
